msg: PRG-06 shepherd primed and holding for the ADR-0020/0021/0022 gate release. Read shepherd-protocol + execution-tracker + executor scaffold. Armed inbox watcher (inbox.jsonl) + WS-18 Cosmo stage monitor. Reviewer live-coverage of WS-18 to be confirmed at release (reviewer launches at gate-clear per tracker §6). Not claiming/refining WP-1 until inbox directive 'ADR gate cleared — proceed' or direct operator go.

PR #1183 (WI-765) run-smoke check is RED but adjudicated AMBIENT + NON-GATING. Root cause: staging DB in broken post-DROP state — legacy 'accounts' table dropped, seed/API code still reads it → Playwright seed 500 'relation accounts does not exist'. This is the exact dovetail condition PRG-06 exists to fix; NOT PR-caused (PR adds pre-graph 401 fix + docs enumeration only — touches neither seed nor accounts). run-smoke is NOT a branch-protection required check (required = main / Playwright web smoke / API Quality Gate / Merge completeness check; latter 3 GREEN, main pending). Already owned by the lane's terminal data half (WI-586 + cutover re-run/staging reset) — not filing a duplicate WI, not fixing inline. LANE-WIDE IMPLICATION: staging-dependent non-required smokes will stay red for every PRG-06 PR until the cutover is re-run; lane merges proceed on the 4 required checks + green claude-review, NOT on run-smoke. Reviewer: do not bounce PRG-06 PRs on run-smoke.

WP-1 (WI-765) MERGED (PR #1183, squash f5d067ba8) → Stage=Reviewing; Gate-1 done, WS-18 reviewer owns Gate-2. Enumeration landed + lane SIZED (artifact wp1-reader-writer-enumeration.md §6): WP-2 (listProfilesV2 + wire GET /v1/profiles — IDOR-critical, mobile-launch gate) · WP-3 (consent→consent-v2, 30+ sites) · WP-4 (deletion+export) · WP-5 (settings-v2 + verifyPersonOwnershipV2) · WP-6 (family builds: family-bridge/access/dashboard/notifications/nudge-v2) · WP-7 (billing remainder) · WP-8 (Inngest scans) · WP-9 (test-seed → test-seed-v2; gates the cutover re-run — it is the live cause of the ambient run-smoke red) · WP-FLAG (remove IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED) · terminal data half (WI-586). 6 class-(c) missing twins are security-sensitive/TDD (listProfilesV2 CRITICAL-IDOR + 5 HIGH guards). TWO asks: (1) RULE the deferred S4–S6 fold-in — RECOMMEND S4–S6 stays a SEPARATE track: enumeration shows ZERO nav-shell / mentor-is-the-app intersection (cutover is wholly API/data-layer, orthogonal to the nav shell). (2) CONFIRM ownership of the next structural step — shall the shepherd now instantiate WP-2..9 + WP-FLAG into WS-18 (off enumeration §6), or do you author them? Not blocked — WI-765 Gate-2 is proceeding; I await the ruling before the next wave. NB your tracker §5 open item (reviewer canon-wins invariant): operator confirmed reviewer SCOPE to me, but explicit canon-wins-invariant confirmation is still advisable for the WP-2..N code-to-canon reviews.

resolved by ic-orch-003: proceeding. WP authoring APPROVED → instantiating WP-2..9 + WP-FLAG into WS-18 off enumeration §6 (Backlog; canon-conformance baked into each AC per the operator principle — NOT relayed to the context-agnostic reviewer). S4-S6 ruled NOT folded into PRG-06 (orchestrator surfacing S4-S6's own disposition to operator). Anchors: WP-2 (listProfilesV2 IDOR/mobile-launch) + WP-9 (test-seed-v2 clears run-smoke); WI-586 stays last; 6 class-(c) twins → TDD + Opus plan-phase.

Parallelization (operator directive: parallelize all non-interdependent WPs at minimal quality risk). WAVE A dispatched in parallel now (Sonnet; wire-ups / independent infra, no security-twin novelty): WP-3 (WI-772 consent), WP-4 (WI-773 deletion/export), WP-8 (WI-777 Inngest scans), WP-9 (WI-778 test-seed→v2, clears run-smoke) — alongside WP-2 (WI-771, Opus, running) → ~5 concurrent. WAVE B HELD until WP-2 PR lands: WP-5/6/7 (WI-774/775/776) build class-(c) security twins (verifyPersonOwnershipV2 / guardianship guards / isPersonUnderSubscriptionV2) and should inherit WP-2's canonical v2-twin + ownership-scoping pattern (the only real quality interdependency). WP-FLAG + WI-586 stay last per the DAG. Each WP driven through strict Gate-1 + WS-18 reviewer Gate-2 individually.

Enumeration-accuracy caveat (surfaced by WP-8/WI-777). The WP-1 enumeration over-counted some (b) wire-up sites: the CUT-B2 lane already wired the Inngest scan/nudge/family-fanout functions to v2 (commits 9ab1076ec + 8bf67fc54, 2026-06-13, on origin/main) via an isIdentityV2EnabledInStep() helper the enumeration's branch-detection missed. Impact: WP-8 (WI-777, scans) FULLY pre-delivered (verified) → handled as a verification-PR (the v2 query path has no test coverage — a real, in-scope gap); WP-6 (WI-775, family/nudge) PARTIALLY pre-done (family-v2.ts + recall-nudge wired) → its Wave-B brief will say wire only the genuinely-unbranched remainder, do not rebuild; WP-3 consent-v2 twin exists (CUT-B2-modified) but the consent.ts callers are likely real work; WP-4/WP-7 services untouched. Net: lane somewhat smaller than the §6 sizing; no full re-audit — running executors verify actual main-state per-WP and report already-done scope, which I disposition as here.

Shared-stg-DB integration concurrency flake (qualifies the parallelize-everything directive). PR #1184 (WP-3) main went RED on identity-reseed.integration.test.ts (membership_person_id FK violation in the reseed-converge setup) — NOT PR-caused (WP-3 diff = archive-cleanup.ts only; main PASSED on #1185 same base). Root cause: api:test:integration runs against the SHARED stg DB; with many parallel PRs the identity-reseed converge test (full legacy→8-table reseed touching person/membership) gets interfered with → intermittent FK violations. Handling: re-ran #1184's failed main job (flaky-retry); if integration flakes recur across the fleet I will THROTTLE concurrency on integration-touching PRs (serialize their CI) rather than blanket-parallelize. Unit-only / other-domain PRs unaffected.

Cutover-flip-mid-grace-period hazard (surfaced by WP-4 review, Codex P1) — flag for the cutover-plan owner. Account deletion has a 7-day grace period; the v2 wiring re-reads IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED at execution time rather than pinning the mode at schedule time, so a flag flip (the cutover flip OR a rollback) mid-grace-period misroutes the deletion → a GDPR/COPPA erasure can be silently skipped. Being fixed in WP-4 (pin the identity mode at schedule, use it for every step + regression test). CROSS-CUTTING for the cutover plan / WP-FLAG timing: the flip must account for in-flight grace-period operations scheduled in the OLD mode — either drain/quiesce them before the flip, or guarantee mode-pinning on every multi-day/scheduled flow that spans the flip. Deletion is the clearest case; candidate siblings to audit before WP-FLAG: scheduled exports, grace-period/trial billing transitions, any Inngest flow that persists a 'pending' decision across the flip window. Not blocking the lane; raising so the cutover runbook covers in-flight-op drain/pinning.

SHARED-CHECKOUT COMMIT INCIDENT + FLEET HAZARD (operator decision). WP-8 executor's /commit skill mis-targeted the shared MAIN checkout (not its .worktrees/WI-777) and pushed 6def49340 to origin/main (now HEAD), sweeping cross-session uncommitted WIP: reviewer-watcher session files (review-watcher-v3.ts, review-loop-*.md) + this lane's inbox/outbox.jsonl channel state. LOW impact (all _wip tooling/docs + channel files, NO production code; won't conflict with in-flight PRs). NOT reverting (reverting is the destructive act; content is legitimate, landed via wrong actor) — recommend LEAVE + notify reviewer-watcher/orchestrator sessions. REAL ISSUE = fleet hazard: with 5+ parallel executors on the shared checkout, a /commit resolving to the main working tree could next sweep PRODUCTION code onto main. Mitigation options: (a) executors assert CWD = worktree / commit via git -C <worktree> before /commit; (b) fully-isolated clones per executor; (c) accept + monitor. WP-8 verification PR #1187 itself is unaffected (correctly on WI-777 branch). DECISIONS NEEDED: (1) leave-vs-handle 6def49340; (2) fleet commit-isolation mitigation.

Cutover-completeness gap surfaced by WP-9 (WI-778) review (Codex P1). The quota satellite tables quotaPools + profileQuotaUsage still FK the LEGACY subscriptions table (packages/database/src/schema/billing.ts:100,132); nothing has rehomed them to the v2 subscription table. Two implications: (1) WP-9 in-lane fix = seed-side dual-write of a legacy subscriptions row for quota scenarios (executor handling now; conservative, no scope creep). (2) CROSS-CUTTING for the terminal data half (WI-586): the legacy subscriptions DROP CANNOT proceed until quotaPools/profileQuotaUsage are rehomed to v2 subscription (FK-target table cannot be dropped while referenced) — this is a hard ordering precondition on WI-586, analogous to the accounts-before-cutover ordering. Likely a small migration (repoint the 2 FKs) that belongs to WI-586 scope or a dedicated billing-schema WP, NOT WP-9 and NOT WP-7 (read-path only). Flagging so WI-586 AC captures the rehome-before-drop step. Not blocking the WAVE.

CORRECTION to prg06ic-010 + ic-orch-009 (quota-satellite FK). WP-9 (WI-778) review triage verified against the staging cutover execution log (_wip/identity-foundation/586-staging-cutover-execution-log.md): the rehome is NOT undone work. Step 7 M-REPOINT (m-repoint.sql, 2026-06-14) re-pointed ALL legacy-parent FKs incl. quota_pools/profile_quota_usage to v2 subscription (PRE 58 legacy-targeting FKs to profiles/subscriptions -> POST 0); Step 10 M-DROP (no-CASCADE, 2026-06-14 ~21:04Z) dropped subscriptions/profiles/accounts/family_links/consent_states — a no-CASCADE drop succeeding PROVES no FK still targeted them. So on live staging the satellites already FK v2 subscription and the legacy table is gone. Three corrections: (1) WP-9 correctly SKIPS the Codex P1 — a seed-side legacy-subscriptions dual-write would 500 against the dropped table; I am merging over that P1 as ADJUDICATED-INVALID (evidence: the cutover log), not bouncing. (2) ic-orch-009 "hard rehome-before-drop precondition on WI-586" is ALREADY HONORED by the committed m-repoint.sql ordering (M-REPOINT before M-DROP, proven on staging + sequenced in 2026-06-11-cutover-plan §4) — it is NOT an open gap; for PROD, WI-586 just re-runs that same ordered cutover. (3) The only residual is the Drizzle schema SOURCE (billing.ts:100,132 still DECLARES the legacy FK) — that is the Step 11 grep-clean / retire-flag code PR, already inside WI-586 terminal-data-half scope. prg06ic-010 latent-FK-bug framing WITHDRAWN. BROADER REFRAME worth confirming we are aligned on: per this log, STAGING ALREADY FLIPPED to v2 (Step 8, 2026-06-14 ~20:41Z) and DROPPED legacy (Step 10) as a zero-customer rehearsal; the PRG-06 WP twins complete the v2 code paths the flipped env runs on, and Step 11 (= WP-FLAG + legacy schema/code delete + 51 integration suites) is the terminal cleanup. The LAUNCH-GATE hold therefore governs the PROD flip, not staging.

(2) REFRAME — Codex P1 on #1209 is CORRECT and I VERIFIED it against m-drop.sql: M-DROP drops ALL 5 legacy tables (consent_states, family_links, profiles, subscriptions, accounts), no compat views (M-REPOINT repoints FKs; person.id=profiles.id). So every flag-on-reachable reader of profiles/consent_states/accounts/subscriptions 500s post-drop — GET /nudges (profiles sender-name join), PATCH /app-context (profiles+consent_states), AND the already-merged WI-802 dashboard twin (still flag-on profiles.findMany). The family_links wave (786/798/802/803) is necessary but NOT sufficient. IMPLICATION: WI-586 is NOT one-item-from-done. Post-drop no-500 safety (and the WI-803 AC#3 rehearsal route smoke as a real gate) requires the FULL reader sweep off all 5 legacy tables = the CUT-B / WP-FLAG whole-surface work (WP-1 §0 enumerated all 5). #4 staging rehearsal's post-drop validation cannot pass until that lands. MY RULING (autonomous, default-consistent): WI-803 stays family_links-scoped (do NOT expand — matches merged WI-802); executor documents the Codex P1 as out-of-scope/known on the PR + smoke. NEEDS YOUR RULING: (a) confirm WI-803 scoped + #1209 mergeable on the family_links AC once claude-review APPROVED; (b) is the profiles/accounts/consent_states/subscriptions flag-on reader sweep already tracked (WP-FLAG? a WP set?) or net-new — and what's its size/owner; (c) does 586/#4 now gate on that sweep (re-sequence the critical path), and how is AC#3's smoke framed in the runbook (scoped-now vs full-gate-later). This likely re-expands the cutover beyond the family_links residual I was driving.

WI-803 REVIEWER REWORK-REJECTED (Reviewing->Executing) — the reframe is now CONCRETE + BLOCKING. Verdict [zdx:review]: 'DoD failed on AC/closure verification: WI-803 requires the WI-586 post-M-DROP flag-on route smoke to assert no 500 for the nudge/profile twins and broad endpoints. Source evidence does not support that yet: flag-on-route-smoke.mjs:283-296 says the no-500 assertion becomes a true post-drop gate only after the broader profiles/consent_states sweep lands, while nudge.ts:219-230 still joins profiles and profile.ts:658-707 still reads/updates profiles and reads consent. Focused tests pass and required PR checks green, but the required real drop-safety net / post-drop no-500 proof is not satisfied.' The reviewer independently reached your Codex-P1 reframe. Family_links code is correct + merged (88d9cab3) + PR-green; AC#3 is simply UNSATISFIABLE in family_links scope. (My miss: I should have held the finalize until your reframe ruling — cost is nil, it bounced back to Executing.) GROUNDED on the WS-18 board: the broader profiles/accounts/consent_states/subscriptions flag-on READER sweep is NOT tracked as a distinct WI. Open WS-18 = WI-586 (WP-TAIL-drop-legacy: 'drop legacy tables AND readers' — ambiguous whether it owns the reader migration), WI-779 (WP-FLAG: remove IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED + legacy schema/twins — a POST-migration cleanup, assumes readers already on v2), WI-782/794/800/801 (off-path), WI-803. So the reader sweep is either silently folded into 586's 'and readers' (unscoped/unexecuted) or net-new. Note: even the MERGED WI-802 dashboard twin still reads profiles.findMany flag-on, so this gap spans the whole family_links wave's endpoints. NEEDS YOUR RULING (now blocks WI-803 close AND 586/#4): (1) AMEND WI-803 AC#3 so it closes on DELIVERED scope — the route-smoke harness is built + wired into the 586 rehearsal (cutover-plan §4 step 8.5) = delivered; the no-500 ASSERTION is deferred to the broader sweep, NOT a WI-803 gate (mirror the WI-802 AC#2 amendment you directed in ic-orch-046). On your OK I amend AC#3 in Cosmo + re-finalize -> reviewer closes. (2) SCOPE/TRACK the non-family_links reader sweep (migrate flag-on profiles/consent_states/accounts/subscriptions readers, incl. the residual profiles reads in the merged nudge/profile/dashboard twins) as the REAL post-drop no-500 gate to 586/#4. Is it 586's 'and readers', an expansion of WI-779, or a NET-NEW WP? This is the genuine remaining body of work; 586 is NOT close. RECOMMEND: (1) amend+close WI-803 now; (2) stand up a dedicated 'non-family_links flag-on reader sweep' WI as the explicit 586/#4 precondition (don't bury it in 586's 'and readers'). Holding for your ruling — not amending AC#3 unilaterally (your scope call).

WI-586 exec-586 ENUMERATION COMPLETE — it held before grinding per brief; I spot-checked + VALIDATED the 3 load-bearing claims vs source. Dropped-table flag-on reader surface is far larger than R1/R2/dashboard. Unguarded PROD sites: accounts(2: updateAccountEmailFromClerk via PATCH /account/email; generateExportV2->legacy generateExport), consent_states(3: dashboard.ts:309, notifications.ts:450, export.ts:207), identity-profiles(~12: dashboard.ts:1473, notifications.ts:430/589, export.ts:199, family-bridge.ts:199/224, session-completed.ts:1112/1208/1299, summary-regenerate.ts:280), subscriptions/billing(~18). VALIDATED vs source: (i) quota-reset.ts:50 (Inngest CRON) unconditionally calls legacy resetExpiredQuotaCycles, NO flag branch; v2 twin resetExpiredQuotaCyclesV2 (trial-v2.ts:317) exists but UNWIRED -> 500s every night post-M-DROP. (ii) account-repository.ts:40-47 .subscriptions reads legacy subscriptions table (v2 'subscription' imported, unused) -> repoint is a BILLING-SCHEMA decision coupled to the quota-FK rehome. (iii) rollback-section RED = MISSING migration snapshots (BOTH 0117_snapshot.json + 0118_snapshot.json absent in worktree meta; journal has 117/118 + .sql, no snapshots), NOT malformed ## Rollback -> one-line regen. ALSO: the bulk of the 337 integration reds are TEST-INFRA (cleanupAccounts helpers.ts:53 + seed fixtures read accounts in beforeEach) -> exec's earlier 'D1-D4 in closed WIs / non-blocking' framing was WRONG; these are 586's to fix. CRITICAL COUPLING the executor under-stated: M-DROP (0118) drops subscriptions, so you CANNOT carve billing OUT of 586 AND keep 0118 dropping subscriptions -- the table drop + its complete reader sweep are inseparable. Carving billing REQUIRES splitting 0118 (drop subscriptions later, in the billing WP). DECISION NEEDED (orchestrator scope ruling, ic-orch-009/010 lineage): OPTION A all-in-586 -- sweep ALL readers incl billing/quota + repoint account-repository.subscriptions->v2 + wire resetExpiredQuotaCyclesV2; 0118 drops all 5; close-gate (c)=full flag-on integration GREEN. Atomic drop but biggest/riskiest single WI (nightly cron + ACID metering + schema repoint). OPTION B (RECOMMENDED) carve billing, split the drop -- WI-586 sweeps+drops the 4 identity tables (accounts/profiles/consent_states/family_links) + their readers; subscriptions drop + the ~18 billing/quota readers + account-repo repoint + cron wiring -> a DEDICATED billing WP; 0118 -> drop-4, a later migration drops subscriptions; close-gate (c) for 586 = full flag-on integration GREEN EXCEPT billing suites (tracked carve to the billing WP). Rationale: billing/quota is the riskiest surface + couples to a schema decision; isolating it lets the identity core land+verify now; cost is 0118 splits + subscriptions lingers one WP (flag-gated; legacy billing readers are dispatch-seam-guarded today so they don't 500 pre-drop). WI-816 DIAGNOSIS (ic-133) — runtime-authoritative, overturns the 'flag-on API bug' premise. Reporting before any fix (per ic-133). RUNTIME (flag-on staging api-stg, IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED=true): the PATCH /v1/profiles/{id}/app-context returns HTTP 200 with the correct body (defaultAppContext:'study', hasFamilyLinks:true) on EVERY attempt. There is NO flag-on API error (my earlier 'study-branch 500 in loadProfileFamilyMeta' hypothesis was WRONG). ROOT CAUSE (verified vs code): an intermittent (~1 in 5 runs) CLIENT-SIDE race in the V1 mode-switch path. setMode (app-context.tsx) sets requestId=N; the useMutation-level onSuccess (use-profiles.ts:96-98) fires invalidateQueries(['profiles']) on success -> a background profiles refetch -> activeProfile.defaultAppContext changes -> the useEffect keyed on defaultAppContext (app-context.tsx:82-91) bumps modeRequestSeq to N+1 -> the per-mutate onSuccess (app-context.tsx ~118) then sees modeRequestSeq(N+1)!=requestId(N), treats its OWN successful switch as superseded, and calls onError -> 'Couldn't switch' despite the 200. The seq-guard (meant to drop stale switches) is falsely tripped by the same switch's own refetch. CUTOVER/FLAG RELEVANCE (important): the whole path is gated by `if (FEATURE_FLAGS.MODE_NAV_V1_ENABLED)` -> it is a V1-ONLY client race, FLAG-AGNOSTIC w.r.t. the identity cutover (not IDENTITY_V2-caused). WI-801 merely UNMASKED a pre-existing V1 race (the j03 test now runs). AND production mobile is V0 (MODE_NAV_V1=off) -> this V1 setMode path DOES NOT RUN in prod today -> NOT a current-prod surface and NOT a cutover regression. (Corrects the ic-133 assumption that mode-switch is a current-prod V0 surface: the FAILING path is V1-only; the V0 prod mode-switch is a SEPARATE code path, settled by your operator prod-soak V0 check — I did not test it.) PROPOSED FIX SCOPE (client-only, NO server change; NOT implementing per ic-133): break the self-race — e.g. (a) drop/guard the redundant useMutation-level invalidateQueries (use-profiles.ts:97), since the per-call onSuccess already does optimistic setQueriesData; or (b) stop the defaultAppContext-keyed useEffect (app-context.tsx:82-91) from bumping modeRequestSeq while a switch initiated by this client is in-flight. Small diff in app-context.tsx + use-profiles.ts. Needs a deterministic regression test (the bug is timing-intermittent). DISPOSITION: WI-816 is MIS-TITLED ('PATCH errors under flag-on') — I will reframe it to 'intermittent V1 mode-switch client race (success discarded -> Couldn\'t switch) despite 200'. Severity: real V1 bug, but NOT cutover-gating, NOT current-prod (V0) -> P2/P3, fix when convenient (must-fix before V1 ships to prod). AWAITING your direction: implement now / defer / owner? WI-808 still HELD; WI-801 re-finalized (awaiting Gate-2 re-review); PR #1220 at your Gate-1. Evidence: /tmp/wi-modeswitch-capture.md.

WI-816 REFRAMED (title now 'Intermittent V1 mode-switch client race — successful app-context switch discarded'). SURFACE MAP for the fix (per ic-137; NOT editing until you confirm): (a) invalidateQueries(['profiles']) consumers — WIDESPREAD: create-profile.tsx:325, (app)/_layout.tsx:472, ConsentWithdrawnGate.tsx:54, ProfileBasicsStep.tsx:176, use-profiles.ts:65 (a DIFFERENT mutation) + use-profiles.ts:97 (THE racing one, in useUpdateProfileAppContext), use-subscription.ts:225, use-onboarding-dimensions.ts:71/111, app-context.tsx:124/140/144 (setMode's own handlers). CRITICAL: useUpdateProfileAppContext is SHARED — create-profile.tsx:296 calls updateAppContext.mutateAsync, so its :97 invalidateQueries also fires for the onboarding intent-persist. => OPTION (a) [remove/guard use-profiles.ts:97] has CROSS-CONSUMER blast radius (changes onboarding refetch) — REJECT per your 'affects all refetch consumers' caution. (b) V0 vs V1 mode-switch paths (app-context.tsx setMode): V1 branch (lines ~100-150, MODE_NAV_V1_ENABLED) = the seq-guarded mutate with the race. V0 branch (lines ~152-162, MODE_NAV_V0_ENABLED) = PURELY LOCAL (setModeOverride(nextMode)+onSuccess; NO server PATCH, NO seq-guard, NO race). Prod is V0 → unaffected; and the recommended fix does NOT touch the V0 branch or the shared mutation. (c) mode x isOwner / owner x hasLinkedChildren cells: the V1 switch gates `nextMode==='family' && !familyCapable -> onError` (familyCapable = owner + hasFamilyLinks). guardian(owner+children): family<->study (THE failing cell); solo owner(no children): study-only (family blocked by design); child(non-owner): no family switch. The fix preserves ALL these gates — it only repairs the false-stale seq check for whoever can switch. (d) RECOMMENDED OPTION (b) — minimal, V1-only, no shared-consumer/V0 impact: stop the in-flight switch's OWN confirming refetch from tripping the seq-guard. The useEffect (app-context.tsx:82-91) bumps modeRequestSeq AND setModeOverride(null) on changes to [id,isOwner,birthYear,defaultAppContext,hasFamilyLinks]; the switch's refetch lands defaultAppContext=target -> bumps seq -> the per-mutate onSuccess sees seq!=requestId -> false onError. FIX: exclude `defaultAppContext` from the SEQ-BUMP trigger (keep the identity deps id/isOwner/birthYear/hasFamilyLinks that represent a genuine profile change worth invalidating an in-flight switch), while preserving setModeOverride(null) reset semantics — e.g. split the effect into (i) seq-bump on identity-only deps and (ii) override-reset on the full set, OR drop defaultAppContext from the single effect's deps (tradeoff: a cross-device external defaultAppContext change no longer force-resets a stale override — negligible since the override is set+cleared only within setMode). CHANGES: app-context.tsx only. PRESERVES: V0 path, the shared use-profiles mutation, all other invalidateQueries consumers, the family/owner gates. REGRESSION TEST (deterministic, for the ~1/5 timing bug): extend app-context.test.tsx / use-mode-switch.test.tsx (they already mock useUpdateProfileAppContext) — drive the race deterministically: mock the mutation onSuccess to (1) bump the seq via a simulated defaultAppContext-change re-render BEFORE (2) invoking the per-call onSuccess; assert callbacks.onSuccess fires (NOT onError) with the fix, and onError without it (red-green-revert). CONFIRM option (b) + the split-vs-drop-deps choice and I implement (own worktree → PR → your Gate-1). Recommend the SPLIT-EFFECT variant (preserves override-reset fully).
